### Post by ff8mania on 2007-05-12
Hi guys,

I've a big problem.

When ubuntu starts it can't automatically mount a my ntfs partition, /dev/sda1, but it mounts all other ntfs partition.

Infact fstab file is a lot strange:

```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda3 :
UUID=5cd3daad-508c-4357-8e4d-5017682a09a6 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda4 :
UUID=256f04b2-337a-4373-b352-b886859e6b32 /home ext3 defaults 0 2
# Entry for /dev/hda1 :
UUID=94D4D87DD4D862D2 /media/hda1 ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda5 :
UUID=A8FD421AD6C0074F /media/hda5 ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hdb1 :
UUID=9E204EBB204E9A63 /media/hdb1 ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/ !! UNKNOW DEVICE !! :
UUID=B8BC2031BC1FE8A0 /media/sda1 ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=29fb6941-4859-458c-a286-e5145d68cd59 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0

```

Why is sda1 called UNKNOW DEVICE? And why its UUID is so different from sda1, sda2 and sda3 ones?

Thank a lot! :)

Bye :D
